summary: Physical assets register — the blue twin of UR050 (white land registry). 115 all-DB (dark blue = objects) cords recording bridges (chaka x3), canals (chana x2), and physical terrains (pata x12). Same collection (Anna Maria Soldi), same vocabulary, same administrator. The secret (paka x6) is much more present than in UR050 (x2) — hidden assets or undisclosed locations. Coverage 79.6%.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  115 cords: 1 INT (value=5), 113 STRING, 1 EMPTY, 1 cluster.
  ALL DARK BLUE (DB) — 113/113 cords. DB = 'objects' in color system.
  Private Collection: Anna Maria Soldi (same as UR050).
  Coverage: 79.6% (90/113 STRING cords confirmed).

PHYSICAL CHARACTERISTICS
  Monochrome dark blue khipu. DB color encodes concrete objects —
  where UR050 (white) recorded abstract rights, UR054 (blue) records
  physical assets: bridges, canals, actual terrain.
  Same collection, same core vocabulary, likely same administrator.

VOCABULARY
  tata (father, title): x13 — the lineage holders
  pata (terrain): x12 — physical land parcels
  papa (father, kinship): x9 — the blood fathers
  wapata (administrator[ACC]): x7 — the administrator as object of record
  paka (hidden/secret): x6 — the secret (3x more than UR050!)
  taka (to stake): x5 — boundary marking
  kaka (maternal uncle): x5 — the guarantor
  way (path): x3 — routes and access
  chaka (bridge): x3 — physical bridges (absent from UR050)
  wapa (administrator): x3 — the managing authority
  chana (canal): x2 — irrigation/drainage canals (absent from UR050)
  pi (who?): x2 — interrogatives
  waka (huaca): x1 — the sacred site
  wama (falcon): x1 — seasonal omen (also in UR055)
  wata (year): x1 — temporal marker

STRUCTURE

  OPENING (cords 1-11)
    papa — the father. taka tata — staking the father's boundary.
    way — the path. patalla — the terrain ONLY (limitative -lla).
    chapapa — of the guardian. GEN-chaka — of the bridge.
    PASS-paka (cord 7) — the hidden[passive]. The secret appears
    immediately, cord 7 of 115.
    wapa-1OBJ — the administrator, ME. Same signature as UR050/UR055.
    tata — the father. 1OBJ-pi — me, who?
    Cord 12: empty — separator.

  BODY (cords 13-55)
    More narrative than UR050's monotone register.
    wama paka (cord 22) — the falcon and the secret. Same omen
    association as UR055 (the oracle). The falcon presages revelation.
    chana (cord 27) — the dried canal. Physical infrastructure.
    kaka-ACC (cord 31) — the uncle[object]. The guarantor intervenes.
    pata-1OBJ (cord 39) — MY terrain. Personal claim.
    ACC-waka (cord 48) — the huaca[object]. The sacred site named.
    taka (cord 53) — to stake. Number 5 (cord 56).

  THE SECRET RETURNS (cords 57-86)
    taka paka (cord 73) — to stake the secret. Marking the location
    of what is hidden. Planting a boundary marker on the secret.
    tata paka (cord 76) — the father's secret. Same phrase as
    UR055 cord 178 (paka tata). The father's hidden designation.
    Four consecutive terrains (cords 80-83): pata-GEN, ACC-pata,
    pata, ACC-pata. A list of specific parcels.
    ACC-paka (cord 86) — the secret as object. Acted upon.

  CLOSING (cords 87-115)
    1OBJ-cha / 1OBJ-cha (cords 87-88) — me[DIM], me[DIM].
    Doubled tenderness. The administrator speaks with emotion.
    ACC-chaka (cord 89) — the bridge[object].
    paka-1OBJ (cord 105) — MY secret.
    chana (cord 106) — the canal.
    kaka-GEN / 1OBJ-chaka (cords 108-109) — of the uncle / my bridge.
    Final sequence: kaka-ACC / tatataka / tatata / kaka-ACC.
    The uncle-father loop closes: uncle, father, father, uncle.

COMPARISON — THE SOLDI DOSSIER

  UR050 (white, 164 cords) vs UR054 (blue, 115 cords):

  |              | UR050 (white)    | UR054 (blue)      |
  |--------------|------------------|-------------------|
  | Color        | W (neutral data) | DB (objects)      |
  | Cords        | 164              | 115               |
  | paka (secret)| x2               | x6 (3x more)     |
  | chaka (bridge)| x0              | x3                |
  | chana (canal)| x0               | x2                |
  | wama (falcon)| x0               | x1                |
  | Tone         | Monotone register| More narrative    |
  | Function     | Abstract rights  | Physical assets   |

  Both from Anna Maria Soldi collection. Same 6-root vocabulary.
  Same administrator signature (wapa-1OBJ). Likely the same official.

  The three-khipu dossier hypothesis:
    UR050 (white) = cadastre of RIGHTS (who owns what)
    UR054 (blue) = register of ASSETS (bridges, canals, land)
    A third khipu (black?) = oracle CONFIRMATION (like UR055)

  The color system encodes document function:
    White = data, abstract records
    Dark blue = objects, physical inventory
    Black (LK) = text, governance, oracular speech

NARRATIVE — THE BLUE REGISTER

  The same hand that tied the white khipu tied this one. The same
  administrator, the same collection, the same six words repeated
  in the same order. But the color is different. Where the white
  register counted rights — lineages, titles, years — the blue
  register counts things. Bridges. Canals. The actual ground.

  It begins the same way: the father, the stake, the path, the
  terrain. But by the seventh cord the secret appears. Something
  is hidden, and it appears three times more often here than in
  the white register. Six times the word paka — hidden, secret,
  concealed — surfaces among the bridges and the canals.

  What is hidden? The bridges tell us. Three bridges (chaka) are
  recorded here that do not appear in the white register at all.
  Two canals (chana) that are absent from the white record. A
  falcon (wama) that presages something. These are physical
  structures — infrastructure — that exist on the ground but
  were not listed in the abstract ledger of rights.

  Cord 73: taka paka — to stake the secret. To plant a boundary
  marker on hidden ground. This is not metaphor. This is a
  surveyor marking a location that the white register does not
  acknowledge. Hidden assets, unrecorded bridges, canals that
  belong to someone but appear in no official tally.

  The father's secret (tata paka, cord 76) reappears — the same
  phrase that closes UR055, the oracle khipu. The father hid
  something, and this blue register is the inventory of what
  he hid. The white register shows what is declared. The blue
  register shows what is real.

  At the end, the uncle closes the record. Kaka-ACC, tatata,
  kaka-ACC — the guarantor witnesses that these hidden assets
  exist, even if the white books do not list them. The
  administrator signs: my secret, my bridge, my canal.

  Together, the white and the blue are a complete portrait of
  an estate: what is public, and what is private. What the
  empire knows, and what the family keeps.
